sections.paragraph:
πλέγμα, ατος, τό (Eur., Pla., X.; Is 28:5 Aq. and Theod.; Philo; Jos., Ant. 2, 220: ‘someth. that is plaited/woven, braided’) braided hair, w. focus on stylish coiffure 1 Ti 2:9.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

someth. someth. = something

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 824
